mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, triggering a sharp repricing of equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust aggregate demand and pricing power, jointly driving positive equity returns. The interaction between demand strength and supply-side capacity utilization amplifies the signal relative to either series alone.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand and supply growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (RSAFS × INDPRO) falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-demand regimes do not exceed baseline by at least 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,250 thousand units annualized, signaling strong residential investment and consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as housing strength propagates to broader economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when HOUST > 1250, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand net new jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness that precedes broader economic slowdown. This threshold-triggered condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk rises.
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite weak payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equity valuations but signals policy lag. The interaction between inflation and policy rate produces positive returns over 20 trading days in this specific regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in this regime do not exceed baseline by at least 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered condition predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as margin compression concerns emerge.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite industrial production decline, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously expand, signaling synchronized demand strength across consumption and investment. The joint signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with broad-based demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (GDPC1 × RSAFS) falls below 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth regimes do not exceed baseline by at least 2.5% annualized.
